etymology.description: 不(不) (bù, "not") negates or indicates nonexistence; 起(起) (qǐ, "rise; start; get up") involves an upward or beginning motion.
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 不(不) (bù, "not") with 起(起) (qǐ, "rise; start; get up") indicates the inability to rise or start, leading to meanings like 'cannot' or 'fail to.' It describes a lack of capability or success in initiating an action or upward movement.

examples.chinese: 这个礼物太贵了，我买不起(這個禮物太貴了，我買不起)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège lǐwù tài guì le, wǒ mǎi bù qǐ.
examples.english: This gift is too expensive, I cannot afford it.

examples.chinese: 房子太大了，我们住不起(房子太大了，我們住不起)。
examples.pinyin: Fángzi tài dà le, wǒmen zhù bù qǐ.
examples.english: The house is too big, we cannot afford to live in it.

examples.chinese: 对不起，我今天不能来(對不起，我今天不能來)。
examples.pinyin: Duìbùqǐ, wǒ jīntiān bù néng lái.
examples.english: I'm sorry, I cannot come today.
